---
title: "The AI-Enhanced Deliverable"
subtitle: "How the Part 1 slide became this website — and exactly how AI was used"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

## What this deliverable is

The hiring exercise asks for an AI-enhanced version of the Part 1 slide, in
a format that can be embedded in a website. **This website is that
deliverable.** The one-page slide
([download](missoula_data_story_slide.pdf)) is the seed; the site enhances
it into something a static slide can't be:

- **The [data story](index.qmd)** expands the slide's narrative into a full
  memo with an executive summary, findings, recommendations, and caveats —
  with a [PDF version](missoula_housing_memo.pdf) built from the same
  source.
- **The [interactive map](map.qmd)** turns the slide's hero map into an
  explorable Leaflet map: hover any hex for its capacity, toggle on the
  3,587 underlying opportunity parcels, click one for its details.
- **[Methods & Sources](methods.qmd)** documents every cleaning decision,
  assumption, and methodological choice — the audit trail a slide can
  never carry.
- **The whole site is embeddable and reproducible**: it renders with
  Quarto from a public repository, republishes automatically via GitHub
  Actions on every push, and every number regenerates from one command
  (`Rscript code/run_all.R`).

## How AI was used

The exercise asks for AI-driven enhancements to the Part 1 deliverable,
and that is where I used it. I designed and built the analysis — the
capacity framework, the opportunity screen and its exclusion rules, the
hot-spot methodology, and the pipeline behind them. **Claude (Anthropic)**
was then used to enhance and harden the deliverable:

- **Enhancing the one-page slide into this website** — converting the
  slide's map, figures, and narrative into the Quarto site you're reading,
  including the interactive Leaflet version of the hero map.
- **Producing the slide layout itself** — the page composition that turns
  the pipeline's figures and numbers into the polished one-page PDF.
- **Auditing the code** — reviewing the pipeline for correctness and
  reproducibility, and checking the cartography programmatically,
  including colorblind-safety validation (deuteranopia, protanopia,
  tritanopia) of the palette before it touched a map.

What stayed human: the choice of story (plan-enabled capacity, not another
choropleth), the framing against the Growth Policy rather than zoning, the
analysis and its assumptions, the exclusion rules and their ethics, the
recommendations, and the final read on every number before it shipped.

## Why this transparency matters

A firm that publishes AI-polished work owes its clients clarity about
where the analysis ends and the polish begins. Everything quantitative on
this site traces to the reproducible pipeline in the
[repository](https://github.com/kaseyzapatka/cascadia); the AI
acceleration is documented here; and the assumptions that drive the
headline numbers are inspectable in one file (`code/00_setup.R`). That's
the standard I'd bring to client work.
